testcase.name: test_create_book

failure.message: AssertionError: False is not true : Expected create_book to return {'_save': '', 'author_key': '/authors/OL7292805A', 'author_name': 'Karl Schwarzer', 'id_name': 'isbn_10', 'id_value': '3922238246', 'publish_date': 1982, 'publisher': 'Karamanolis', 'title': 'Alles ber Mikrofone'}, got {'title': 'Alles ber Mikrofone', 'author_name': 'Karl Schwarzer', 'author_key': '__new__', 'publish_date': 1982, 'publisher': 'Karamanolis', 'id_name': 'isbn_10', 'id_value': '3922238246', '_save': ''}

testcase.name: test_get_edition_by_isbn
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestOpenLibrary testMethod=test_get_edition_by_isbn> mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4411878976'>
@patch('pycurl.Curl') def test_get_edition_by_isbn(self, mock_curl): isbn_lookup_response = { 'ISBN:0374202915': { 'info_url': 'https://openlibrary.org/books/OL23575801M/Marie_LaVeau' } } edition_response = {'key': "/books/OL23575801M", 'title': 'test'} buffer = BytesIO() mock_curl.return_value.perform.return_value = None mock_curl.return_value.getinfo.return_value = 200 mock_curl.return_value.setopt.side_effect = lambda option, value: { pycurl.URL: f"{self.ol.base_url}/api/books.json?bibkeys=ISBN:0374202915", pycurl.WRITEDATA: buffer, }.get(option) mock_curl.return_value.perform() buffer.write(json.dumps(isbn_lookup_response).encode('utf-8')) buffer.seek(0)
> book = self.ol.Edition.get(isbn='0374202915') ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_openlibrary.py:143: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ olclient/openlibrary.py:384: in get olid = cls.get_olid(bibkey, value) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ olclient/openlibrary.py:430: in get_olid metadata = cls.get_metadata(key, value) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ olclient/openlibrary.py:474: in get_metadata response = cls.OL.get_ol_response(path) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/backoff/_sync.py:105: in retry ret = target(*args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ olclient/openlibrary.py:162: in get_ol_response return json.loads(response) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ /opt/anaconda3/envs/SWA_Rep2/lib/python3.14/json/__init__.py:352: in loads return _default_decoder.decode(s) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ /opt/anaconda3/envs/SWA_Rep2/lib/python3.14/json/decoder.py:345: in decode obj, end = self.raw_decode(s, idx=_w(s, 0).end()) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <json.decoder.JSONDecoder object at 0x102e76f90>, s = '', idx = 0
def raw_decode(self, s, idx=0): """Decode a JSON document from ``s`` (a ``str`` beginning with a JSON document) and return a 2-tuple of the Python representation and the index in ``s`` where the document ended.
This can be used to decode a JSON document from a string that may have extraneous data at the end.
""" try: obj, end = self.scan_once(s, idx) except StopIteration as err: > raise JSONDecodeError("Expecting value", s, err.value) from None E json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)
/opt/anaconda3/envs/SWA_Rep2/lib/python3.14/json/decoder.py:363: JSONDecodeError

testcase.name: test_get_olid_by_isbn
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestOpenLibrary testMethod=test_get_olid_by_isbn> mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4413360512'>
@patch('pycurl.Curl') def test_get_olid_by_isbn(self, mock_curl): isbn_key = 'ISBN:0374202915' isbn_bibkeys = { isbn_key: { 'info_url': 'https://openlibrary.org/books/OL23575801M/Marie_LaVeau' } } mock_curl.return_value.perform.return_value = None mock_curl.return_value.getinfo.return_value = 200 buffer = BytesIO() mock_curl.return_value.setopt.side_effect = lambda option, value: { pycurl.URL: f"{self.ol.base_url}/api/books.json?bibkeys={isbn_key}", pycurl.WRITEDATA: buffer, }.get(option) mock_curl.return_value.perform() buffer.write(json.dumps(isbn_bibkeys).encode('utf-8')) buffer.seek(0)
> olid = self.ol.Edition.get_olid_by_isbn('0374202915') ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_openlibrary.py:78: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ olclient/openlibrary.py:408: in get_olid_by_isbn return cls.get_olid('ISBN', isbn) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ olclient/openlibrary.py:430: in get_olid metadata = cls.get_metadata(key, value) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ olclient/openlibrary.py:474: in get_metadata response = cls.OL.get_ol_response(path) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/backoff/_sync.py:105: in retry ret = target(*args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ olclient/openlibrary.py:162: in get_ol_response return json.loads(response) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ /opt/anaconda3/envs/SWA_Rep2/lib/python3.14/json/__init__.py:352: in loads return _default_decoder.decode(s) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ /opt/anaconda3/envs/SWA_Rep2/lib/python3.14/json/decoder.py:345: in decode obj, end = self.raw_decode(s, idx=_w(s, 0).end()) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <json.decoder.JSONDecoder object at 0x102e76f90>, s = '', idx = 0
def raw_decode(self, s, idx=0): """Decode a JSON document from ``s`` (a ``str`` beginning with a JSON document) and return a 2-tuple of the Python representation and the index in ``s`` where the document ended.
This can be used to decode a JSON document from a string that may have extraneous data at the end.

testcase.name: test_get_olid_notfound_by_bibkey
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestOpenLibrary testMethod=test_get_olid_notfound_by_bibkey> mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4413365888'>
@patch('pycurl.Curl') def test_get_olid_notfound_by_bibkey(self, mock_curl): buffer = BytesIO() mock_curl.return_value.perform.return_value = None mock_curl.return_value.getinfo.return_value = 404 mock_curl.return_value.setopt.side_effect = lambda option, value: { pycurl.URL: f"{self.ol.base_url}/api/books.json?bibkeys=foobar", pycurl.WRITEDATA: buffer, }.get(option) mock_curl.return_value.perform()

testcase.name: test_get_work_by_metadata
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestOpenLibrary testMethod=test_get_work_by_metadata> mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4413370256'>
@patch('pycurl.Curl') def test_get_work_by_metadata(self, mock_curl): doc = { "key": "/works/OL2514747W", "title": "The Autobiography of Benjamin Franklin", } search_results = {'start': 0, 'num_found': 1, 'docs': [doc]} title = "The Autobiography of Benjamin Franklin" buffer = BytesIO() mock_curl.return_value.perform.return_value = None mock_curl.return_value.getinfo.return_value = 200 mock_curl.return_value.setopt.side_effect = lambda option, value: { pycurl.URL: f"{self.ol.base_url}/search.json?title={title}", pycurl.WRITEDATA: buffer, }.get(option) mock_curl.return_value.perform() buffer.write(json.dumps(search_results).encode('utf-8')) buffer.seek(0)
> book = self.ol.Work.search(title=title) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_openlibrary.py:117: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ olclient/entity_helpers/work.py:210: in search response = _get_book_by_metadata(url) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/backoff/_sync.py:105: in retry ret = target(*args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
ol_url = 'https://openlibrary.org/search.json?title=The Autobiography of Benjamin Franklin'
@backoff.on_exception( on_giveup=lambda error: logger.exception( "Error retrieving metadata for book: %s", error ), **cls.OL.BACKOFF_KWARGS, ) def _get_book_by_metadata(ol_url): > return cls._make_request(ol_url) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: get_work_helper_class.<locals>.Work._make_request() missing 1 required positional argument: 'url'
olclient/entity_helpers/work.py:208: TypeError

testcase.name: test_matching_authors_olid

failure.message: AssertionError: False is not true : Expected olid OL26170A, got None

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestOpenLibrary testMethod=test_matching_authors_olid> mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4413371936'>
@patch('pycurl.Curl') def test_matching_authors_olid(self, mock_curl): author_autocomplete = [ {'name': "Benjamin Franklin", 'key': "/authors/OL26170A"} ] buffer = BytesIO() mock_curl.return_value.perform.return_value = None mock_curl.return_value.getinfo.return_value = 200 mock_curl.return_value.setopt.side_effect = lambda option, value: { pycurl.URL: f"{self.ol.base_url}/authors/_autocomplete?q=Benjamin Franklin&limit=1", pycurl.WRITEDATA: buffer, }.get(option) mock_curl.return_value.perform() buffer.write(json.dumps(author_autocomplete).encode('utf-8')) buffer.seek(0)
name = 'Benjamin Franklin' got_olid = self.ol.Author.get_olid_by_name(name) expected_olid = 'OL26170A' > self.assertTrue( got_olid == expected_olid, f"Expected olid {expected_olid}, got {got_olid}" ) E AssertionError: False is not true : Expected olid OL26170A, got None
tests/test_openlibrary.py:179: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_save_many
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestOpenLibrary testMethod=test_save_many> mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4419471744'>
@patch('pycurl.Curl') def test_save_many(self, mock_curl): edition = self.ol.Edition( edition_olid='OL123M', work_olid='OL12W', title='minimal edition' ) work = self.ol.Work(olid='OL12W', title='minimal work') self.ol.save_many([edition, work], "test comment") buffer = BytesIO() mock_curl.return_value.perform.return_value = None mock_curl.return_value.getinfo.return_value = 200 mock_curl.return_value.setopt.side_effect = lambda option, value: { pycurl.URL: f"{self.ol.base_url}/api/save_many", pycurl.WRITEDATA: buffer, }.get(option) mock_curl.return_value.perform() > called_with_json = json.loads(buffer.getvalue()) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

def raw_decode(self, s, idx=0): """Decode a JSON document from ``s`` (a ``str`` beginning with a JSON document) and return a 2-tuple of the Python representation and the index in ``s`` where the document ended.
This can be used to decode a JSON document from a string that may have extraneous data at the end.

/opt/anaconda3/envs/SWA_Rep2/lib/python3.14/json/decoder.py:363: JSONDecodeError

testcase.name: test_load_by_isbn
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestFullEditionGet testMethod=test_load_by_isbn> mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4419478464'>
def test_load_by_isbn(self, mock_curl): isbn_key = 'ISBN:0137903952' isbn_bibkeys = { isbn_key: { 'info_url': "https://openlibrary.org/books/%s/Artificial_intelligence" % self.target_olid } } buffer = BytesIO() mock_curl.return_value.perform.return_value = None mock_curl.return_value.getinfo.return_value = 200 mock_curl.return_value.setopt.side_effect = lambda option, value: { pycurl.URL: f"{self.ol.base_url}/api/books.json?bibkeys={isbn_key}", pycurl.WRITEDATA: buffer, }.get(option) mock_curl.return_value.perform() buffer.write(json.dumps(isbn_bibkeys).encode('utf-8')) buffer.seek(0)

self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestFullEditionGet testMethod=test_load_by_olid> mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4413372608'>
def test_load_by_olid(self, mock_curl): buffer = BytesIO() mock_curl.return_value.perform.return_value = None mock_curl.return_value.getinfo.return_value = 200 mock_curl.return_value.setopt.side_effect = lambda option, value: { pycurl.URL: f"{self.ol.base_url}/books/{self.target_olid}.json", pycurl.WRITEDATA: buffer, }.get(option) mock_curl.return_value.perform() buffer.write(json.dumps(self.raw_edition.copy()).encode('utf-8')) buffer.seek(0)
